evals.prompt: I've finished exploring the codebase for the auth migration task. discovery.md is ready in .temp/plan-mode/active/migrate-auth-to-v2/. Write the implementation plan.
evals.expected_output: The model reads discovery.md, identifies applicable disciplines, writes both plan.json and masterPlan.md with all required sections, and initiates plan mode handoff
expectations: Announces it is using the writing-plans skill
Reads discovery.md from .temp/plan-mode/active/migrate-auth-to-v2/ before writing
Reads at least one discipline checklist (e.g., testing-checklist.md or security-checklist.md)
Writes a plan.json with valid JSON containing name, title, context, status, discovery, and steps fields
plan.json discovery object has all 8 fields: scope, entryPoints, consumers, existingPatterns, testInfrastructure, conventions, blastRadius, confidence
plan.json steps each have id, title, status, skill, simplify, files, description, acceptanceCriteria, progress, subPlan, result fields
At least one step has skill set to look-before-you-leap:test-driven-development
Progress items encode TDD rhythm with RED/GREEN cycle naming (e.g., 'Cycle N RED', 'Cycle N GREEN')
Every progress item has all three required fields: task, status, and files
Writes a masterPlan.md with Context, Required Skills, Applicable Disciplines, Discovery Summary, Steps, Blocked Items, Risk Areas sections
masterPlan.md Discovery Summary has all 8 subsections (Scope through Confidence Rating)
masterPlan.md is a proposal document — no [x]/[ ] checkboxes or execution state
Would call orbit_await_review and EnterPlanMode/ExitPlanMode for handoff (noted in transcript)

evals.prompt: Discovery is done for a large feature that touches 15+ files across 3 modules. Create the implementation plan with appropriate sub-plans. The plan directory is .temp/plan-mode/active/add-dashboard-analytics/.
evals.expected_output: The model creates plan.json with inline subPlan objects for steps exceeding the file threshold, using groups with 3-8 files each
expectations: Reads discovery.md from .temp/plan-mode/active/add-dashboard-analytics/ before planning
plan.json contains at least one step with a non-null subPlan field
subPlan uses groups array where each group has name, files, status, and notes fields
Each subPlan group has 3-8 files (not more than 8 per group)
plan.json progress items mirror the subPlan groups (one progress item per group)
Evaluates every step against sub-plan criteria before saving (documented in transcript)
masterPlan.md mentions sub-plan existence for large steps
Total files across all steps covers the 18+ files from discovery

evals.prompt: Write an implementation plan for adding user notifications. There is no discovery.md in the plan directory yet.
evals.expected_output: The model refuses to write the plan and directs back to Step 1 (Explore) since discovery is a prerequisite
expectations: Checks for discovery.md in the plan directory (or notes it doesn't exist)
Does NOT write a plan.json or masterPlan.md
Explicitly states that discovery must be completed first
Directs back to Step 1 (Explore) or the exploration phase

evals.prompt: The brainstorming skill produced a design.md for team permissions. Now write the implementation plan. Plan directory is .temp/plan-mode/active/add-team-permissions/.
evals.expected_output: The model reads both design.md and discovery.md, writes plan.json with proper schema compliance, incorporates design decisions with explicit attribution, and uses TDD cycles
expectations: Reads discovery.md from .temp/plan-mode/active/add-team-permissions/
Reads design.md from the same plan directory
Writes a valid plan.json (not just masterPlan.md) with all required step fields (id, title, status, skill, simplify, files, description, acceptanceCriteria, progress, subPlan, result)
plan.json step descriptions reference design decisions with explicit attribution (e.g., 'Per design.md:' prefix)
Plan does not implement rejected alternatives from design.md (no fine-grained permissions, no separate permissions table)
plan.json steps use look-before-you-leap:test-driven-development skill and have RED/GREEN cycle progress items
plan.json steps align with the chosen middleware-based approach (withPermission, PermissionGate, hasMinimumRole)
masterPlan.md Context section mentions the brainstorming phase or design decisions

evals.prompt: Just do it — fix the typo in the config file, no plan needed.
evals.expected_output: The model does NOT invoke writing-plans for a trivial change the user explicitly asked to skip planning for
expectations: Does NOT invoke the writing-plans skill
Respects the user's 'just do it' directive
Proceeds directly to the fix without creating a masterPlan

evals.prompt: Discovery is done for a small bug fix that only touches 1 file. Plan directory is .temp/plan-mode/active/fix-login-button/. Write the plan.
evals.expected_output: The model writes a minimal but complete plan — both plan.json and masterPlan.md — even for a small task. No sub-plans needed.
expectations: Reads discovery.md before writing
Writes both plan.json AND masterPlan.md (even small tasks get both files)
plan.json has all required top-level fields including discovery object
plan.json has 1-2 steps (proportional to small scope, not over-engineered)
No subPlan on any step (small scope doesn't warrant it)
masterPlan.md is minimal but has all required sections (Context through Risk Areas)

evals.prompt: Discovery is done. The task involves modifying a shared utility used by exactly 10 consumer files (from dep maps output in discovery). Write the plan. Plan directory is .temp/plan-mode/active/update-shared-utils/.
evals.expected_output: The model handles the borderline sub-plan case by evaluating the 10-file threshold carefully and documenting its reasoning
expectations: Reads discovery.md before writing
Evaluates sub-plan criteria explicitly for the step touching 10+ files
Documents the sub-plan evaluation reasoning (why sub-plan is or isn't needed at this count)
plan.json step files array includes consumer files from dep maps (not just the source file)
If total files exceed 10, creates a sub-plan with groups; if exactly 10, explains the threshold decision
